claim: The lab's strongest wedge is acting as a transfer gate, not simply as a score generator.
mechanism: Scores tell maintainers where they stand, but transfer gates tell them which lessons deserve to propagate across chips. When the lab stores mechanism-rich packets and only promotes rules after repeated wins, it converts evaluation work into portfolio leverage and makes each future chip benefit from prior evidence.
boundary: This wedge only materializes if promotion standards remain strict and packet quality stays high.
